extraction.one_line_summary: VAN GARDEREN Tejay won from a select group after DUMOULIN Tom survived repeated QUINTANA attacks through sheer time-trial power bridging gaps.
tactical_patterns: Dumoulin repeatedly bridged using TT power after groups splintered rather than matching explosive accelerations, preserving energy while nullifying attacks.
Quintana attacked multiple times but lacked final punch to distance Dumoulin, revealing weakness in sustained climbing power versus anaerobic bursts.
GC group stalemate allowed escapees Van Garderen/Woods to gain time, punishing defensive racing by Nibali/Zakarin who refused to chase.
Zakarin attacked late gaining 42 seconds on stalled leaders, showing opportunistic tactics work when favorites mark each other.

rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: defended pink jersey without teammates by tactically bridging gaps using TT strength rather than matching accelerations, showing race-winning patience
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: multiple attacks failed to drop Dumoulin despite isolation, suggesting insufficient sustained power to capitalize on numerical disadvantage
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: positive: outsprinted Woods after both gained time on deadlocked GC group, showing opportunistic racing beats conservative group-watching
rider_signals.ZAKARIN Ilnur: positive: late solo attack gained 42 seconds moving to 1:58 down, exploiting GC leaders' mutual neutralization
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: refused to work in final despite being down on GC, allowing riders behind to close gap through inaction
